title: angelica dahurica
intro: Angelica dahurica, also known as Bái zǐ rén or Dahurian angelica, is a type of perennial plant that belongs to the carrot family (Apiaceae) and is native to the mountainous regions of China, Japan, and Korea. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antispasmodic, and diuretic effects. The health benefits of Angelica dahurica include relieving menstrual cramps, reducing fever, and alleviating digestive issues, as well as acting as a natural remedy for hypertension and respiratory problems. In traditional herbal preparations, the plant's roots are often used to make teas, infusions, or decoctions, which can be consumed to harness its medicinal properties.
